citation.description: TestKernelCoachWidening.test_mixed_block_and_advisory_yield_tagged_entries verifies 2 entries with decision='block'/'advisory' (case a); test_block_only_zero_advisory_still_coaches verifies 1 entry with decision='block' (case b). guide_url for blocking is verified in test_plan_review_coach_renders_blocking_note_with_decision (line 157); advisory guide_url pre-exists in test_plan_review_explain.py. The implementation in coach.py:226-235 computes coachable = list(blocking)+list(surviving) and builds decision_map.

criteria.criterion: A plan review whose verdict contains blocking findings emits a coaching entry (move_id + rendered note + guide_url) for each blocking finding. Oracle: two unit test cases - (a) 1 block + 1 advisory yields 2 coaching entries with decision tags and guide_url; (b) 1 block + 0 advisories yields 1 coaching entry with decision='block' and guide_url.

citation.description: TestYamlArmIdentity.test_both_arms_coach_blocks_are_binding_identical parses plan-review.yaml and asserts both arms' coach_inputs and coach_gate bindings are equal. The YAML itself (plan-review.yaml lines 174-228 and 254-308) confirms both arms have identical blocking/surviving/findings bindings. Test is in tests/unit/ rather than tests/unit/workflow/ as the oracle suggests, but the substance is fully verified.

criteria.criterion: The two YAML arms' coach blocks remain binding-identical after the edits. Oracle: unit test in tests/unit/workflow/ parsing plan-review.yaml and asserting then-arm and else-arm coach_inputs/coach_gate/coach_notes/coach step bindings are equal.

citation.description: TestWorkflowOps.test_coach_inputs_emits_coachable_union stubs StepContext with 1 blocking + 1 surfaced finding and asserts findings=[fb,fa] and both ids in instructions. test_plan_review_coach_renders_blocking_note_with_decision asserts decision='block' and guide_url on the blocking coaching note. Tests are in tests/unit/ rather than tests/unit/workflow/ but substance is verified. workflow_ops.py:360-380 shows plan_review_coach_inputs accepts blocking input and emits coachable union.

criteria.criterion: The workflow path produces the same widening. Oracle: unit test (tests/unit/workflow/) stubbing a StepContext for plan_review_coach_inputs with 1 blocking + 1 surfaced finding, asserting the findings output contains both and the instructions listing names both; and a plan_review_coach test asserting a note referencing the blocking finding renders with decision='block'.

citation.description: plan_review_coach.md line 13 says 'For each finding in the list below' (not 'advisory finding'). coach.py line 123 uses '## Surviving findings (by id)' (not 'advisory findings'). test_listing_header_is_decision_neutral in test_coach_blocking_findings.py:74-77 asserts the header contains '## Surviving findings (by id)' and 'advisory findings' is absent.

criteria.criterion: The coach prompt and listing header no longer frame the input set as advisory-only. Oracle: grep -niP 'advisory\s+finding' plan_review_coach.md returns no hits, AND a unit test asserts the rendered listing header for a mixed set is decision-neutral ('Surviving findings').

citation.description: TestSidecarAndCli.test_coaching_decision_survives_build_payload calls build_payload with a coaching entry having decision='block' and asserts payload['coaching'][0].get('decision') == 'block'. sidecar.py lines 516-528 include decision in the coaching persistence dict.

criteria.criterion: Coaching entries for blocking findings survive sidecar persistence with their decision tag. Oracle: unit test over build_payload asserting a coaching entry with decision='block' retains that field in the emitted payload.

citation.description: test_subject_validator_applies_to_blocking_notes verifies that an imperative subject ('add a retry loop') is rejected even when the only input is a blocking finding, returning notes == []. The subject validator in coach.py:148-159 is unchanged and applies to all findings via render_coach_notes.

criteria.criterion: Blocking-finding coaching notes pass the same C1 subject validation as advisory notes. Oracle: existing subject-validator tests extended to a blocking-finding case; suite green.

citation.description: test_cli_renders_blocking_coaching_line_with_guide_url calls _render_plan_review_text with a BLOCK verdict containing a coaching entry with decision='block' and guide_url, then asserts the coaching text and guide_url appear in the output. _llm_commands.py:567-569 iterates verdict['coaching'] and renders coaching text + guide_url for all entries.

criteria.criterion: rebar review-plan output for a BLOCK verdict includes the blocking finding's coaching line + guide link in the existing flat coaching block. Oracle: CLI rendering unit test over _render_plan_review_text asserting the rendered text contains the blocking finding's coaching text and guide_url.

citation.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart records: locally at commit 52d7a3dc1 'make lint' showed 1022 files formatted + ruff clean, 'make typecheck' showed 'Success: no issues found in 326 source files', focused suites ran 10+151 passed green (2026-07-15), full 'make test' 6012 passed. Authoritatively, Gerrit change 708 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/708) earned CI Verified +1 and LLM-Review +1, merged on main at commit 52d7a3dc1. The codebase shows real, non-stubbed implementation consistent with this attestation.

criteria.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test all pass.
